title: Camera-trap mesh coverage from a single ranger camp tower (LoRa + LTE-CatM1 + VHF backup)
summary: A ranger camp hosts a multi-link tower carrying three concurrent radios: a LoRa 868 gateway for the primary camera-trap mesh, an LTE Cat-M1 modem for higher-bandwidth devices that can reach a cell tower nearby, and a 150 MHz VHF antenna for backup wildlife-collar reception. Op C runs the propagation pipeline once per radio and produces a per-link breakdown plus a combined site score weighted by the operator's priorities. The scenario shows how heterogeneous links share one site without the request having to know how each link's plugin computes its outputs.
demonstrates: rx_templates with one entry per distinct link_type

sites.name: ranger-camp-letaba-tower
sites.notes: 30 m self-support, mains+solar, on the existing supply road.

notes: Each Tx is paired with the rx_template whose link_type matches its radio's link_type. Because the tower's `default_equipment_refs[]` already lists the three radios, the request omits `tx_equipment_refs` and lets the engine resolve them from the Site. The `combined_site_score` weights the LoRa link highest because the camera-trap mesh is the gating tier of the deployment; LTE is a convenience path; VHF is backup. Combined output uses `weakest_link` as the gating factor by default.
